title: Lemony Mustard-Crusted Salmon & Potatoes
summary: Zingy mustard salmon, buttery spuds and tender asparagus, all baked in parchment for a no-fuss midweek dinner that feels a bit fancy.

method.description: Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Slice the lemon in half, then cut a few thin half-moon slices from one half. Keep the other half for serving. Trim the woody ends off the asparagus and cut in half if needed. Drain the potatoes and slice them in half. Roughly chop the dill.
Season the salmon fillets with salt and black pepper. Spread over the wholegrain mustard, dot with the butter and top each fillet with a few lemon slices.

Lay out one large rectangle of baking paper (per person), portrait. Arrange the potatoes, asparagus and capers in single layer on the bottom half of the paper leaving a 10cm gap between the edge closest to you and the food, then top with the salmon. Drizzle with the olive oil, season with salt and black pepper, and mix everything together. Fold the top half of the baking paper over the food then fold the outside edges over each other to seal (like a cornish pasty), creating a steam-tight parcel. Repeat with the remaining ingredients.If you’re scaling up for more people, just use a parchment-lined baking tray and a tight foil lid instead. Cooking times might vary, so keep an eye on it.
Pop onto a baking tray and slide into the oven for 15–20 mins, or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily.

Remove from the oven and carefully open the parcels and lift out the salmon and set aside. Stir the chopped dill through the potatoes and asparagus. Serve everything up with the remaining lemon for squeezing over.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
